cuts.note: How I get my morning tech briefing without opening a single tab.
Stanley scanning Hacker News - headlines, scores, comments...
10 top stories. Saved. Ready to read. 4 seconds.
Stop refreshing Amazon waiting for a price drop. Let Stanley watch it.
Scanning listing - current price, original price, savings badge...
Price logged at $279.99. $70 off list. Watchlist updated.
Stop manually filtering job boards every morning. This alone takes hours.
Sweeping Indeed for remote UX Designer roles with salaries listed...
8 positions saved. Titles, companies, salary ranges - all captured.
I used to check 3 competitor pricing pages every week by hand.
Extracting team plan pricing from Monday, Asana, and Notion...
Monday wins by $1.99/seat. $597/yr saved at 25 seats. Logged.
5 new Google reviews came in overnight. Stanley caught all of them.
Scanning Maps listing - rating, reviewer names, and full review text...
5 reviews saved with star ratings. Negative feedback flagged.
I fill out this exact same 9-field form every single week. Not anymore.
Stanley filling vendor timesheet - name, ID, project code, hours, description...
Submitted. Confirmation logged. Took under 5 seconds.
Before every outbound email I run the same company research checklist.
Scanning Wikipedia infobox - founding year, HQ, CEO, leadership...
Company profile built. Ready for outreach. One prompt, done.
